title: Blow-up in finite time for the dyadic model of the Navier-Stokes equations

def:3.1.summary: Weak solution: H-valued function u(t) with $u_n \in C^1$ satisfying the dyadic ODE (eq:3.1) for all n.

def:3.2.summary: Leray-Hopf solution: weak solution satisfying energy inequality (eq:3.3) for a.e. t₀.

thm:4.1.summary: Existence of weak solutions via Galerkin approximation. Energy inequality holds for a.e. t₀. Classical NSE proof adapted to dyadic setting.

thm:4.2.summary: Nonnegativity preservation: $u_n(0) \ge 0 \Rightarrow u_n(t) \ge 0$ for all t. Energy inequality holds for ALL t₀ (no exceptional set). Key insight: no backward energy transfer.

thm:4.3.summary: Local regularity for $\alpha > 1/3$. Enstrophy estimate gives Riccati inequality for ‖u‖^2, bounded for short time.

thm:4.4.summary: Global regularity for $\alpha \ge 1/2$. Enstrophy Riccati inequality + energy inequality $\Rightarrow$ contradiction with blowup.

lem:5.1.summary: Sobolev inversion: for $\alpha < 1/3, \sum\lambda^{(1+2\gamma)n}|u_n|^3 \ge$A‖u‖$_{\alpha+\gamma}^3$. Uses Hölder with p=3, q=3/2. The exponent gap $\varepsilon = 2-6\alpha-2\gamma > 0$ is what makes $\alpha < 1/3$ critical.

lem:5.2.summary: Continuity of the cross-term $\sum\lambda^{2\gamman}(u_n u_{n+1})(t)$. Needed to ensure the Lyapunov function H(t) is continuous.

thm:5.3.summary: MAIN BLOWUP: for $\alpha < 1/3$, large initial data $\Rightarrow$ ‖u‖$^3_{1/3+\gamma}$ not locally integrable. Proof: define Lyapunov H(t) = ‖u‖$^2_\gamma +$ c₂$\sum(u_n u_{n+1})$, show dH/dt ≳ $H^{3/2}$ via Sobolev inversion, Riccati comparison $\Rightarrow$ finite-time blowup.

def:6.1.summary: Attracting set and global attractor definitions in strong/weak metrics.

thm:6.2.summary: Weak global attractor exists. If trajectories are compact, it equals the maximal invariant set.

cor:6.5.summary: For $\alpha \ge 1/2$, weak attractor is a strongly compact strong global attractor.

edges.note: Constructs a weak solution in the sense of def:3.1
Shows the constructed solution is Leray-Hopf (energy inequality a.e.)
Uses existence from Galerkin (thm:4.1), then strengthens to energy inequality for all t₀
Applies Galerkin + enstrophy estimates to get local strong solutions
Energy inequality (all t₀) contradicts Riccati blowup of ‖u‖^2 for $\alpha \ge 1/2$
Explicitly cited: $'u_n(t) \ge 0$ for all n,t>0 due to Theorem 4.2'. Nonnegativity is essential for the product estimates.
Sobolev inversion gives $\sum\lambda^{(1+2\gamma)n}u_n^3 \ge$A‖u‖$^3_{\alpha+\gamma}$, converting the nonlinear term into $H^{3/2}$ growth.
'Thanks to Lemma 5.2, H(t) is continuous on $[0,\infty)'$. Continuity needed for the Riccati comparison argument.
Compactness of trajectories gives maximal invariant set characterization
Global regularity for $\alpha \ge 1/2$ needed to upgrade inequality to equality
Energy equality + continuity in H $\Rightarrow$ strong attractor

layer_1_foundations.description: Existence and structural properties of solutions

layer_3_blowup.description: Blowup below $\alpha = 1/3$ via Lyapunov function / Sobolev inversion

layer_4_attractor.description: Global attractor theory (weak for all $\alpha$, strong for $\alpha \ge 1/2)$

waleffe.title: On some dyadic models of the Euler equations

katz-pavlovic.title: Finite time blow-up for a dyadic model of the Euler equations
